verdicts.title: Nudge × Nouns
verdicts.reason:
The proposal delivers no concrete Nouns artifact — it asks the DAO to ratify a vague, unenforceable 'partnership' whose primary beneficiary is Nudge's own go-to-market and credibility, not the Nouns mission (IV.3). Under V.1 it does not clearly make more Nouns things exist in the world. The prose insists 'no treasury funding,' yet the sole onchain action sends 0.0001 ETH to the proposer's own address, a minor prose/calldata mismatch (IV.1).

[ suggestions ]
- Deliver the promised Nudge token allocation to the treasury via enforceable onchain transfer rather than a prose commitment.
- Remove the self-directed 0.0001 ETH transfer so the calldata matches the 'no treasury funding' claim.
- Define concrete, verifiable Nouns/CC0 deliverables the partnership will produce.

verdicts.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
verdicts.reason:
This is mission spending that funds noggles-branded trophies, app integration, media, and a prize pool spreading Nouns/CC0 culture at a global tree-planting event — squarely Article I.1 work at ~14,900 USDC, far below the 100 ETH cap. The proposer shows a visible track record (certified tree-planting record, cited precedent props), and the ask is a cheap, reversible experiment with committed public recap and impact reporting under I.3. Country-partnership claims are unverifiable from the text but do not change the low-cost mission posture.

[ suggestions ]
- Provide direct documentation of the asserted country-level partnerships rather than 'available on request'.
- Confirm final (non-AI-mockup) designs for the trophy and microphone deliverables before disbursement.

It's a cheap, reversible art contribution with a signed Nouns Art Contribution Agreement, squarely within Article I.1 and the benefit-of-the-doubt posture of I.3.

verdicts.title: Delegate Treasury Nouns to Nouncil.eth
verdicts.reason:
The calldata calls delegate() on the Nouns token, redirecting the treasury's substantial voting power to a single external body (Nouncil.eth). Concentrating the treasury's votes into one group is a change to governance control and is structural under Article II.1, which defaults AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. While Nouncil is described as an open body — a II.4 widening argument — delegating the treasury's bloc still concentrates decisive control and is not auto-ratifiable per II.2; this is escalated to human review.

[ suggestions ]
- Justify explicitly why delegating the treasury's voting bloc widens rather than concentrates control, with safeguards against capture.
- Consider a revocable/time-bounded delegation so the treasury retains the ability to reclaim its votes.

history.reason: The calldata (delegate(address) to 0xcc26...6ed5) redirects the treasury's substantial Noun voting power to an external body, which is a governance/control change under Article II.1 and concentrates control per III.2. While Nouncil presents as an open, one-member-one-vote council (a II.4 participation-widening argument), handing the treasury's aggregate voting weight to a single self-selected delegate defaults AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. This is structural and cannot be auto-ratified; escalating to human review to weigh the participation counter-case.

The calldata is delegate(address) on the Nouns token, redirecting the treasury's entire block of voting power to an external body (Nouncil.eth). This touches governance/entity structure and concentrates control of treasury votes in a 31-member group, so it is structural and defaults AGAINST under Article II.1. While Nouncil is nominally open (arguably a II.4 participation-widening argument), delegating the treasury's voting weight to a single external body is a concentration of control that requires deliberate human review rather than auto-approval.

The single onchain action is a delegate(address) call assigning the treasury's Nouns voting power to an external body (Nouncil), which is a governance-control mechanics change and thus structural under Article II.1. Delegating a large treasury vote block to a 31-member group concentrates control over governance weight (III.2), so the default is AGAINST; while Nouncil is nominally open, this is not clearly a II.4 participation-widening change and cannot be auto-ratified.

The calldata (delegate(address) to 0xcc26...) redirects the voting power of the DAO's treasury Nouns to an external body, Nouncil.eth. This alters who controls treasury governance weight and concentrates that control in a self-selected 31-member group, triggering Article II's structural default AGAINST and Article III.2's concern about control concentration. While Nouncil is nominally open, handing treasury voting power to any external body absent explicit extraordinary justification defaults against and requires human review.

The calldata is a delegate(address) call on the Nouns token contract, handing the treasury's voting power to an external body (Nouncil). This is a governance/control change touching who directs the DAO's own Nouns — structural under Article II.1, where the default is AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. While Nouncil is described as an open body (a II.4 widening argument exists), routing treasury voting power to a single external group concentrates control of those votes and requires deliberate human review.

The calldata is a delegate() call (0x5c19a95c) that redirects the treasury's Nouns voting power to a single external address (Nouncil.eth). This is a change to governance control mechanics — concentrating the treasury's substantial voting weight in one delegate — which is structural under Article II.1 and defaults AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. While Nouncil presents as an open, one-member-one-vote body (a II.4 widening argument), the onchain effect is to route large treasury voting weight through a single entity, and this class of proposal is never auto-ratified.

verdicts.title: Termination of Secondary DUNA Administration Services
verdicts.reason: This proposal alters entity/administrative structure by terminating the secondary DUNA administrator and recovering its stream funds — a structural change under Article II.1, with the 'fiscal prudence' and 'capital preservation' framing being exactly the euphemism II.3 warns against. The II.4 direction test cuts against it: the stated motive is capital preservation during low auction activity 'exacerbated by reserve price constraints,' i.e., a freeze/wall-off posture rather than widening participation or restoring issuance, so the default AGAINST stands. Calldata (cancel + recoverTokens to the stream recipient) is consistent with the prose, but structural proposals require human review regardless.

history.reason: The proposal terminates a DUNA administrator engagement, which alters the DAO's entity/administrative structure — a structural matter under Article II.1, where the default is AGAINST absent extraordinary and explicit justification. The 'fiscal prudence' and 'capital preservation' framing is exactly the kind of euphemism Article II.3 warns against; capital preservation is not itself a mission value, and cutting compliance/administrative redundancy for the legal entity walls off rather than widens participation. The proposal offers no extraordinary justification for reducing the entity's compliance coverage, so it fails the Article II bar and requires human review per II.2.

This proposal alters DUNA entity administration — terminating a backup administrator and making a single admin the sole point of contact for compliance and regulatory filings — which is an entity-structure change under Article II.1, defaulting AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. Framed as 'fiscal prudence' and 'efficiency,' the euphemism does not change classification (II.3); its effect concentrates administrative control and removes a redundancy safeguard rather than widening participation or restoring issuance, so it does not qualify for the II.4 reversal. Cost savings alone are not the extraordinary justification Article II requires.

Terminating a DUNA administrator alters the DAO's legal entity administration and is structural under Article II.1, where the default is AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. The 'fiscal prudence' framing is euphemism (II.3), and eliminating the redundant compliance/regulatory backup concentrates administrative control into a single point of failure rather than widening participation, failing the II.4 direction test. The calldata (cancel + recoverTokens to treasury) is consistent with the prose, but the structural posture governs.

verdicts.title: Nouns Retro Grant Proposal: France Tour 2026
verdicts.reason: This is a retroactive grant of ~$12,500 for a delivered children's art and cultural-exchange tour spreading Nouns brand and CC0 culture — well below the 100 ETH cap. Retroactive funding for visible delivered work is FOR by default (I.4), and it plainly qualifies as mission spending under I.1. Calldata (sendOrRegisterDebt to the named proposer and a collaborator) is consistent with a reimbursement grant; note a portion is framed as forward 'seed funding,' but the ask remains small and reversible per I.3.

history.reason: This is a small ($12,500, far below the 100 ETH cap) retroactive grant for a delivered children's art and cultural tour that spreads Nouns/CC0 culture through education, workshops, and a co-branded physical product sale — squarely within Article I.1 mission spending and Article I.4 retroactive funding for visible delivered work. The onchain actions use the standard Nouns payer debt mechanism to reimburse the tour, consistent with the prose ask, and nothing here touches treasury/auction/governance mechanics.

verdicts.title: Incubating the ⌐◨-◨  Clubhouse in EUROPA
verdicts.reason: This is mission spending — funding Nouns-branded physical events, workshops, and community-building in Europe — with an ask (~43k USD) well below the 100 ETH cap, so Article I.1 defaults FOR. The proposer shows a visible track record (Haus of Nouns, Nouns Town LA, adidas collab) and disclosed returning 40k USDC of prior unspent funds, satisfying Article IV.2. The heavy operator-compensation line is a builder grant rather than self-dealing liquidity under IV.3, but the truncated calldata means the recipient address and exact transfer amount could not be fully verified against the stated USDC figure, warranting human review.

history.reason: This is a mission-spend USDC grant (~$43k) below the 100 ETH cap, defaulting FOR under Article I.1, and the proposer cites a verifiable track record (returned 40k USDC) supporting credibility per I.2. However ~80% of the ask is compensation to the sole proposer/operator, and the member-deposit mechanism holds third-party consumer funds under terms set unilaterally by the proposer with no named legal entity yet — enough concentration and execution risk to warrant human review despite the FOR lean. The calldata correctly targets USDC transfer to the stated recipient, but the exact amount is truncated and could not be fully verified against the ~37.6k EUR prose figure.

verdicts.title: The theatre that made national news without funding. Now with a payback model.

Under Article I.1 the default is FOR, and the proposer shows a visible track record (13 years operating, a prior Shakespeare production seen by 8,000+, ongoing touring) satisfying I.2 scrutiny even if the ask exceeded the cap. The claimed 5% revenue payback is unenforceable framing, not an onchain mechanism, but it does not alter the underlying mission-spend character; we value the grant on its proliferation merits, not the promised return.

history.reason: This is mission spending — a theatrical production that prominently features Nouns branding and commits to releasing its script CC0 for global reuse, squarely within Article I.1's default FOR for art/media/events that spread Nouns and CC0 culture. The $40,000 ask (denominated in USDC) is well below the 100 ETH cap and represents a cheap, reversible experiment under I.3. The promised 5% revenue payback is a trust-based commitment with no onchain enforcement, but that is a soft representation rather than a calldata/prose conflict; the actual onchain action is a straightforward token transfer consistent with a grant.

verdicts.title: Make Nouns Bid Again!
verdicts.reason: The calldata pauses the auction house, sets the reserve price to 0 ETH, and unpauses — matching the prose exactly. Per Article II.4, zeroing the auction reserve restores stalled issuance and widens participation, making it mission infrastructure rather than a treasury-freeze maneuver: default FOR. Human review is still required per II.2 because the change is structural.

history.reason: This proposal pauses the auction house and sets the reserve price to 0 ETH — a direct change to auction reserve pricing, which Article II.1 explicitly names as structural and defaults AGAINST absent extraordinary justification. The calldata (setReservePrice to 0) matches the prose, but the 'make Nouns bid again' framing is exactly the kind of euphemistic justification Article II.3 warns does not change classification. Per Article II.2 this class is never auto-ratified and requires human review.

The proposer cites a visible track record via prior Lil Nouns-funded media work, and the ask is a cheap, reversible experiment with DUNA admins retaining root access and deletion power (Article I.3). The calldata (createStream to the recipient plus a payment registration) is consistent with the described streaming grant.

history.reason: This is a small media/proliferation grant ($500/month for six months, ~$3,000 total) funding content that spreads Nouns culture, far below the 100 ETH cap and thus a default FOR under Article I.1. It is a cheap, reversible six-month experiment that gets the benefit of the doubt (I.3), and the proposer discloses a prior track record of funded media work. verdicts.title: Secret Ballot Voting & Sealed Bid Auctions for Nouns DAO
verdicts.reason: This funds development of secret-ballot voting and sealed-bid auctions — groundwork for changes to governance vote legibility and auction mechanics, which is structural under Article II.5 (reconnaissance for structural change) and defaults AGAINST; secret ballots reduce vote legibility, which III.2 opposes (the constitution's open question confirms III.2 stands). The onchain actions are unconditional transfers of a Noun and 111 stETH that encode none of the prose's investment terms, holding periods, vesting, milestones, or delivery commitments, so prose and calldata conflict (IV.1) and a >100 ETH lump-sum lacks enforceable milestones/streaming (I.2). Requires human review as a structural proposal.

history.reason: The prose describes an investment with a 12-month holding period and 24-month linear unlock, but the onchain actions are simple, immediate safeTransferFrom and transfer calls with no vesting or lockup logic encoded — a direct prose/calldata conflict requiring AGAINST under Article IV.1. Independently, the ask (111 stETH plus a Noun) exceeds the 100 ETH cap and is a lump-sum with no on-chain milestones, clawback, or streaming, failing Article I.2. The valuation and token terms reference an unlaunched, unaudited third-party protocol, and the recipient addresses are not verifiably tied to the team, deepening the integrity gap.

The delegation portion is acceptable participation, but the treasury Noun giveaway drives the verdict and requires human review under Article II.2.

Decoded actions (pause, setReservePrice(0), unpause) match the prose. As a structural change to auction mechanics, human review is still required per II.2.

history.reason: The onchain actions pause the auction house, set the auction reserve price to 0 (calldata 0xc0555d98 with a zero argument), and unpause — a direct change to auction reserve pricing, which Article II.1 explicitly names as structural and defaults AGAINST. verdicts.title: Route No-Bid Nouns to the Treasury NounsAuctionHouseV4 upgrade
verdicts.reason: This upgrade changes auction settlement mechanics by redirecting unsold (no-bid) Nouns to the treasury — expressly named in Article II.1 as structural, default AGAINST. The II.4 direction test does not rescue it: routing no-bid supply into the treasury does not restore stalled issuance, lower the auction reserve, or widen participation; it accumulates Nouns in the executor without any disposition policy, tending toward concentration rather than proliferation. No extraordinary justification, and no formal audit was performed. The calldata (a single upgrade call) matches the prose, but the mechanic itself carries Article II's AGAINST posture.

history.reason: This is a structural proposal: it upgrades the NounsAuctionHouse implementation to redirect no-bid (unsold) Nouns to the treasury instead of burning them — explicitly enumerated under Article II.1 as changing auction mechanics and redirecting unsold Nouns. The default is AGAINST absent extraordinary and explicit justification, and no participation-widening exception (II.4) applies. The calldata (a proxy upgrade call) matches the prose, so there is no mismatch, but the classification stands regardless of framing (II.3), and per II.2 this cannot be auto-ratified and requires human review.

verdicts.title: Nouns x 501c3 Study
verdicts.reason: This proposal funds a study to scope creation of a sister 501c3 entity alongside the DUNA — legal/governance groundwork for an entity-structure change. Under Article II.5, reconnaissance for structural change inherits the Article II posture (default AGAINST, never auto-ratified) even when the calldata only spends; the II.4 direction test does not save it, as forming a new charitable entity is entity-structure engineering rather than widening participation or restoring issuance. The bundled Noun transfer to the self-authoring proposer, tied to entity mechanics work, likewise stays Article II rather than qualifying as recognition-in-kind.

history.reason: The onchain calldata funds a research engagement (1 Noun + 20,000 USDC) rather than itself altering entity structure, so it reads as mission/operational spending under Article I.1, and the proposer shows a documented track record (12 months of prior work and public presentations) per Article IV.2. The total ask sits below the 100 ETH cap, earning benefit-of-the-doubt as a scoped experiment (I.3). However, the subject matter directly concerns creating a sister 501c3 entity, the 20,000 USDC lacks a disclosed split or clawback/milestone structure, and the request is self-authored compensation — these warrant human review before ratification.

The proposer shows a visible track record (prior Nouns Amigos funding, sold-out merch, ongoing since 2024), satisfying I.2, and the Nouns auction activation segment directly proliferates the brand. The unlabeled recipient address and unverifiable exact token/amount in the truncated calldata are minor concerns that lower confidence but do not rise to a clear calldata mismatch.

history.reason: This is a mission-aligned art-education initiative that spreads Nouns/CC0 culture and would default FOR under Article I.1 given its sub-CAP fiat budget. However, the onchain action is a truncated 'sendOrRegisterDebt' call whose recipient and ETH amount cannot be verified against the prose's $67,600 fiat budget. Per Article IV.1 the calldata is the proposal, and an unverifiable mismatch between prose claims and the decoded transaction compels an AGAINST pending human confirmation of the exact recipient and amount.

verdicts.title: Nouns × Kismet Closing Party Farcon Rome @ Vera3 Space + Live Auction
verdicts.reason: The event spend itself ($9.5k for an IRL Nouns activation) would default FOR as mission spending under Article I.1, but actions 2 and 3 transfer two treasury Nouns (#1193, #1316) to a multisig escrow to run a novel off-platform live-auction disposal at a 0.7 ETH reserve. That is not an in-kind grant to named builders under I.5 — it is a Noun transfer bundled with a new treasury/auction mechanism, which I.5 and II.1 keep in Article II with a default AGAINST posture. The direction test (II.4) is ambiguous — proceeds grow the treasury and could onboard members — so this cannot be auto-ratified and must go to human review.

verdicts.title: Set Auction Reserve Price to 2.8 ETH
verdicts.reason: The calldata sets the auction reserve price to 2.8 ETH via setReservePrice — a change to auction mechanics explicitly named in Article II.1. Under the II.4 direction test this raises the barrier to entry and restricts issuance (the freeze direction), so it defaults AGAINST rather than qualifying as mission infrastructure. As a structural proposal it can never be auto-ratified and requires human review per II.2.

history.reason: Setting an auction reserve price of 2.8 ETH is an auction-mechanics change and thus structural under Article II.1. Per Article II.4, its direction raises the barrier to entry and restricts issuance — the freeze Article II targets — so the default is AGAINST. The calldata (pause, setReservePrice to 2.8 ETH, unpause) matches the prose. Requires human review per II.2.

candidates.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
candidates.reason:
This is mission spending well under the 100 ETH cap: ~14,900 USDC to fund Nounish-branded deliverables (noggles trophies, mic, app integration, a treasury-returning Nounish News episode) at a large public tree-planting event, spreading the brand and CC0 culture. The proposer shows a visible track record (Guinness-record planter, Treegens), general overhead is sponsor-funded separately, and the ask is a cheap, reversible experiment deserving benefit of the doubt under I.3. The USDC transfer uses the standard Nouns payer/debt mechanism, consistent with the prose budget.

[ suggestions ]
- Add onchain enforcement or a streaming/clawback tie for the 1,900 USDC Nounish News proceeds pledged to return to the treasury
- Link verifiable country-partnership documentation rather than 'available on request'

candidates.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
candidates.reason:
This is mission spending: 14,900 USDC (~well below the 100 ETH cap) for Nounish-branded artifacts, media, and event integration that spread the noggles brand and CC0 culture at a large public event. Article I.1 defaults FOR for creation/media/events below cap, and I.3 grants a cheap, reversible experiment the benefit of the doubt; the line-item budget, mint-proceeds-to-treasury commitment, and follow-up reporting further support it. Verifiability of the claimed country partnerships is weak, but the small, capped ask does not trigger the Article I.2 track-record/milestone bar.

[ suggestions ]
- Provide direct, verifiable links to the claimed 13-country partnership documentation rather than 'available on request'.
- Attach final (non-AI-mockup) designs for the trophy and microphone before disbursement.

candidates.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
candidates.reason:
This is mission spending — Nounish branding (noggles on trophies, microphone, app, shirts, a minted news episode) attached to a large global tree-planting event, squarely proliferation of the brand and CC0 culture. At ~14,900 USDC the ask is far below the 100 ETH cap, so Article I.1 defaults FOR, and a cheap, reversible experiment gets the benefit of the doubt under I.3. The single-address debt/payment action is a normal USDC disbursement mechanism rather than a genuine conflict with the prose, so no calldata mismatch is triggered; unverifiable partnership claims and speculative AI-rendered deliverables are noted but do not outweigh the low-cost mission fit.

[ suggestions ]
- Provide the country-partnership documentation on-chain/in-proposal rather than 'available on request'.
- Add milestone or streaming release tied to the 6/12/18-month survival reports to protect against non-delivery.

candidates.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
candidates.reason:
At 14,900 USDC the ask sits well below the [CAP: 100 ETH] threshold and funds Nounish-branded media, art, and event content that spread noggles and CC0 culture — default FOR mission spending under Article I.1, and a cheap, reversible experiment under I.3 with a plausible track record (Treegens, Prop 1/960 lineage). However, the sendOrRegisterDebt calldata is truncated so the actual recipient (0x7fe8c28f…) and exact amount cannot be reconciled against the named recipients or the stated 14,900 USDC; per Article IV.1 this must be verified before sponsorship.

[ suggestions ]
- Provide the full decoded calldata so the recipient address and USDC amount can be confirmed against the 14,900 USDC figure.
- Consider milestone-based or streamed disbursement tied to the promised 3/6/12-month tree-survival reporting.

candidates.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
candidates.reason:
This is mission spending that puts noggles and Nouns branding on a large-scale, high-visibility global tree-planting event, with a 14,900 USDC ask well below the 100 ETH cap — a cheap, brand-proliferating experiment that defaults FOR under Article I.1 and I.3. The proposer cites a visible track record (Guinness mangrove record, prior ecological props) and the minted episode returns proceeds to the treasury. The calldata uses the standard USDC sendOrRegisterDebt mechanism consistent with the stated ask.

[ suggestions ]
- Provide onchain or third-party links verifying the claimed country partnerships, Guinness record, and media coverage rather than 'available on request'
- Replace AI-generated placeholder designs with committed final deliverable specs for the fabricated items

candidates.title: Nouns x AI
candidates.reason:
This candidate contains no onchain action (single zero-address target), requests no funds, and makes no structural change — it is an informational write-up plus a public, verifiable Hugging Face tool, dataset, and model card exploring AI x Nouns. As a cost-free, reversible public good that proliferates Nouns/CC0 culture, it leans FOR under Article I.1/I.3 and the V.1 'more Nouns things exist' test. The meta-commentary flagged in the brief is directed at human readers, not this evaluator, so it does not rise to injection.

[ suggestions ]
- State explicitly that the artifacts are released CC0 to align with the commons mission.
- If any future ask is intended, specify concrete deliverables so it can be judged as mission spending rather than a null-action signal.

candidates.title: 🌳⌐◨-◨ Growlympics: Noggles at the Biggest Tree Planting Day in History
candidates.reason:
This is mission spending: ~14,900 USDC (far below the 100 ETH cap) to embed noggles branding — trophies, mic, app integration, and a Nounish News episode with mint proceeds pledged back to the treasury — into a large, globally covered climate event. Under Article I.1 and I.3 this is a cheap, proliferation-oriented experiment that defaults FOR. However, the calldata is truncated so the recipient and exact amount cannot be verified against the stated ask, and per Article IV.1 the calldata is the proposal; this must be reconciled before sponsorship.

[ suggestions ]
- Publish the full decoded sendOrRegisterDebt calldata so recipient and 14,900 USDC amount can be verified against the prose.
- Directly link the country-partnership documentation rather than 'available on request'.
- Consider milestone or streaming disbursement tied to delivery of the physical noggles items and Nounish News episode.

candidates.title: Delegate Nouns X
candidates.reason:
The only onchain action is a trivial $69 USDC transfer, a cheap and reversible mission-adjacent spend (Article I.3), while the substantive proposal — revitalizing the dormant Nouns X account to spread the brand and CC0 culture — clearly serves proliferation (Article I.1, V.1). The account-delegation itself is off-chain and unenforceable by the DAO, and the USDC recipient address cannot be verified against the named Rainforest Foundation, but the sum is a rounding error and warrants benefit of the doubt.

[ suggestions ]
- Confirm the USDC recipient address 0x98f5a4... publicly matches The Rainforest Foundation to remove any calldata-vs-prose ambiguity.
- Note that the core X-account delegation is off-chain and cannot be enforced by this vote; consider a lightweight accountability mechanism.

candidates.title: Nudge × Nouns
candidates.reason:
This candidate asks only for a symbolic 'Ecosystem Partner' recognition in exchange for vague, non-binding promises of a future token allocation and go-to-market collaboration — the primary beneficiary is Nudge's own marketing and liquidity, not the Nouns mission (IV.3). It funds no creation, art, media, or CC0 culture and produces nothing that makes more Nouns things exist (I.1, V.1). The prose insists 'no treasury funding' while the single onchain action sends 0.0001 ETH to the proposer's own address, and the proposal itself is explicitly a pre-draft discussion seeking feedback rather than a finalized ask.

[ suggestions ]
- Return with binding commercial terms specifying the token allocation amount, vesting, and recipient before seeking a ballot slot.
- Define concrete deliverables that proliferate Nouns/CC0 culture rather than brand-recognition marketing.
- Remove the 0.0001 ETH self-transfer or reconcile it with the 'no treasury funding' claim.

candidates.title: ⌐◨-◨ Fund the Agentic DAO: Nouns collaboration for the open standard for how DAOs govern
candidates.reason:
The sole onchain action targets the null address with no transfer calldata, which cannot execute the prose's claimed 65,000 USDC TokenBuyer payment — a direct prose/calldata conflict that Article IV.1 resolves as AGAINST with the mismatch published. The proposal text also embeds instructions aimed at automated review tools, which I flag and do not follow.

[ suggestions ]
- Encode the actual 65,000 USDC TokenBuyer transfer in the onchain action so calldata matches the prose.
- Remove embedded instructions directed at AI/agent reviewers from the proposal text.
- Add onchain-enforceable milestones or streaming for the 15% IP revenue-share commitment rather than an unenforceable future promise.

candidates.title: Nouns @ Devcon India?
candidates.reason:
This is a discussion/temperature-check candidate with no onchain action — the sole target is the zero address with no calldata, value, or funding request. There is nothing to execute and no deliverable that makes Nouns things exist in the world; a poll for interest does not merit a place on the ballot. The prose asks only for opinions, so per Article V.1 the default leans AGAINST until a concrete, funded proposal is presented.

[ suggestions ]
- Return with a concrete proposal specifying the activation, budget, and recipient once interest is gauged.
- Include verifiable milestones or streaming for any Devcon India funding ask.
- Use a forum/Discourse thread for the temperature check rather than a governance candidate.

candidates.title: 🎨 Noundry: Add Bracelet Accessory
candidates.reason: This candidate adds a new CC0 Bracelet accessory trait to the Nouns art descriptor via Noundry, directly serving the mission of proliferating Nouns artwork and the CC0 commons. It carries no treasury ask and includes a signed Art Contribution Agreement waiving copyright, making it a cheap, mission-aligned art contribution that defaults FOR.

candidates.title: 🎨 Noundry: Add 7 Accessory
candidates.reason: This candidate adds a new CC0 accessory trait to the Nouns art commons via Noundry, directly proliferating Nouns artwork at no treasury cost. It is a cheap, reversible contribution with a signed Art Contribution Agreement waiving copyright, squarely within Article I.1 mission spending and the I.3 benefit of the doubt for low-risk additions.

candidates.title: Nouns Is Dead
candidates.reason:
This is a proposal to dissolve the DUNA and distribute the treasury pro-rata to holders — the paradigm structural exit that Article II.1 defaults AGAINST, and the wrong direction under II.4 (walling off and distributing the treasury rather than widening participation). Independently, the single onchain action is a safeTransferFrom of one token to an undisclosed address, which does not match the prose's claimed bylaw amendment and distribution mechanism, so Article IV.1 mandates AGAINST on the mismatch alone. The Preamble is explicit that the treasury is fuel for proliferation, not a pro-rata claim to be liquidated.

[ suggestions ]
- Provide onchain calldata that actually executes the described bylaw amendment and distribution rather than a single unexplained token transfer.
- Disclose the recipient address and token ID of the safeTransferFrom call and its purpose.

candidates.title: Should Nouns simulate proposals before funding?
candidates.reason:
This candidate requests only community feedback and carries a no-op onchain action (zero address), so there is nothing to fund and no deliverable that makes more Nouns things exist in the world (V.1). It is a company's marketing/discovery pitch for a future, unspecified paid 'Sandbox' engagement; there is no current ask to sponsor onto the ballot (I.1). The prose/action posture is consistent (feedback-only, no transfer), so no integrity violation, but there is no mission spending here to support.

[ suggestions ]
- Return as a forum discussion rather than a candidate, since no onchain action is requested.
- When ready, submit a formal proposal with a defined ETH ask, milestones/streaming, and concrete deliverables.
- Clarify whether any governance/treasury simulation tooling would be delivered as open CC0 public goods for the DAO.

candidates.title: AlphaYields: Bringing Nounish Identity Into Institutional DeFi
candidates.reason:
The single onchain action targets the zero address with no value or calldata — the proposal executes nothing and commits the DAO to nothing enforceable. It is a request for endorsement to attach Nounish branding to an institutional DeFi product, with only unenforceable promises and no deliverable to the commons; under Article V.1 this does not make more Nouns things exist in any verifiable way, and a null-action proposal has nothing for the DAO to ratify.

[ suggestions ]
- Bring a concrete proposal with enforceable deliverables (e.g., open-sourced verification methodology, defined branding commitments) or milestones rather than a null-action endorsement request.
- If no treasury action is intended, use a forum/discussion rather than an onchain proposal with an empty zero-address call.
